rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF HUGH L. HORTON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 09/13/2022, as no amendment or amended petition has been filed to address the following issues raised in the tentative ruling for the 07/27/2022 hearing: 1) The Court has been requiring a $1,500 minimum cash reserve deemed unavailable for payment of compensation of the conservator and attorneys since the decision of the Conservatorship of A.B. Should the Court adjust the cash reserve upward in light of the recent increase of the Medi-Cal resource limit from $2,000 to $130,000 (as of 07/01/2022)? A specific amount of the cash reserve must be proposed, together with an explanation as to why the proposed amount is appropriate, taking into consideration the conservatee's future cash needs and particular financial circumstances among other factors set forth in Probate Code section 2942(b) and A.B. 2) A specific amount of compensation of the conservator and her attorneys which can be paid out of the estate or set-aside account balance must be proposed, together with an explanation as to why the proposed amount does not impose an economic hardship on the estate, taking into consideration the conservatee's future cash needs and particular financial circumstances among other factors set forth in section 2942(b) and A.B. 3) A specific amount of funds the conservator seeks to set aside to preserve the conservatee's eligibility for public benefits must be proposed, together with an explanation as to why the proposed amount should be set aside in light of the increased Medi-Cal resource limit.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GEORGE STANLEY CORNS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/19/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: The proof of service filed on 5/10/22 is incomplete at item 4a. Either certified copies of Letters issued to the personal representative of a post-deceased heir's estate or an affidavit, pursuant to Probate Code Sect. 13100, must be filed to confirm distribution to heirs of a post-deceased heir. Exhibit G to the petition does not appear to include all interested parties; Decedent's surviving siblings are missing. (The administrator is holding estate funds in a bank not in California.) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 10/10/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARY K. PETERS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. No SASE was provided in which to return a conformed copy of the order. (The dates of the attorney costs do not appear to be accurate or preceded the initiation of these proceedings. Attorney costs should be fully itemized to show any filing fee separately from any costs paid to a service to file a paper.) NB: In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate a proceeding must begin with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution," or "Order Terminating Conservatorship [or Guardianship or Trust]." [LRSF 14.36 I.6.b.] Please make a note of it. Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LOUIS BERT ROVENS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/27/2022 for the same issues. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) This matter will have to go on for hearing when the other issues are resolved because there appears to be a conflict with the proposed personal representative opening up an estate to sue themselves as personal representative. 2) Petitioner did not select in the caption authorization to administer under the IAEA. 3) Must file Proof of Publication (Probate Code 8120 et seq.) 4) Must give 15 days' notice and file DE-121. 5) Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed with a cover page, separately, and confidentially. Do not file form DE-147S as an attachment to the Duties and Liabilities form.[LRSF 14.18 E.] 6) Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. 7) Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 9/12/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CHI KIN WAI
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing to admit the will. No appearance required. Status on close by 8/29/2023. BOnd is set at $20,000.00. Isidoor Bornstein is appointed probate referee.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CHUANG MEI LIU
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/11/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially with a cover sheet. No courtesy copies of the petition, proof of service, or other papers have been received yet by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B. and C.] In hopes of not continuing the hearing, the petition was reviewed online as a ONE-TIME courtesy and the following issues were noted: Item 5a(7) or (8) of the petition must be answered. Item 8 of the petition must clearly identify the trustee of the devisee trust. If the trustee is the petitioner, all trust beneficiaries must be listed and must receive notice. No declination by the first named executor has been filed yet or attached to the petition. Not all other papers have been reviewed yet (e.g., proof of publication). Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 9/30/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
